success_criteria.name: Organizer activation
success_criteria.indicator: Organizers can create a trip, configure visibility, and publish a contribution-ready page without external tooling.

success_criteria.name: Contribution conversion
success_criteria.indicator: Supporters can understand the trip page and complete contributions with minimal friction.

success_criteria.name: Expense transparency
success_criteria.indicator: Members can see clear trip-level expense status and understand event-level balances separately.

success_criteria.name: Receipt usability
success_criteria.indicator: Users can upload, correct, and understand receipts even when source language or currency differs from the trip default.

success_criteria.name: Member participation
success_criteria.indicator: Members actively contribute receipts, expenses, photos, and private event activity rather than relying only on the organizer.

early_metrics: trip creation to member invite completion rate
public trip page to contribution conversion rate
percentage of expenses with attached receipts
manual correction rate after OCR extraction
translation or currency override usage rate
private event creation rate per active trip
measurement_assumptions: Users will engage more if receipts and balances are easier to understand across languages and currencies.
Public supporter trust improves when spending visibility and updates are consistent.
Private event functionality reduces noise in the main trip ledger.
validation_targets: Clarity of public versus private trip surfaces
Comprehension of source versus translated receipt data
Understanding of trip ledger versus private event ledger separation
